title: Senior Visual Brand Designer
description: Senior Visual Designer -  Klimt & Design Klimt & Design is a branding and product design agency specializing in building brands for technology companies from pre-seed and Series A to larger, more mature enterprises. Our clients range from high-growth startups to established global enterprises across sectors such as AI, enterprise SaaS, fintech and healthcare, and our work spans brand creation, rebranding, landing pages, marketing design, and product design. We are seeking a full-time, fully remote, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. The Role We are seeking a full-time, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. This is a client-facing delivery lead position where you will own multiple accounts end-to-end. You will execute the heavy-lifting strategic brand explorations while managing, mentoring, and delegating production tasks to our highly capable mid-level design team. Responsibilities Take ownership of client visual brand design projects, acting as the senior creative that clients experience as the lead. Lead the exploratory zone of brand systems and refreshes, executing competitive research, mood boards, positioning, and defining look, feel, and tone. Present explorations and application mockups to clients on review calls, guiding the team through incorporating feedback successfully. Delegate the extension of the design system (e.g., sizing ads, building out website pages, creating presentation decks) to mid-level designers. Lead daily team standups and act as the first point of contact for mid-level designers needing direction or unblocking. Own final quality assurance, catching issues at your level and ensuring strict process discipline for file hygiene and versioning. Anticipate and flag project risks (timeline, scope, missing inputs) and come to the table with proposed solutions. Oversee and occasionally execute sales enablement collateral (such as pitch decks and slide templates), defining the visual strategy before delegating the broader build-out to the team. Requirements Proven experience working autonomously in a senior design role, comfortable defining a brand system from very little initial direction. A standout portfolio demonstrating core competency in visual identity, brand design, and excellent creative thinking. High comfort level running client review calls and articulating and defending design decisions. Strong ability to context-switch across multiple accounts – ranging from earlier-stage tech companies to mature clients – at different stages of delivery. Proficiency in Figma and all industry-standard design tools. A strong commitment to mentorship; making the people around you better must be viewed as a core part of the job, not a bonus. Excellent fluency in English (verbal, comprehension, and communication). A mindset of "initiative over permission," where you proactively ask questions to fill gaps and recommend solutions rather than waiting to be told what to do. To apply, please submit a link to your portfolio or a few work samples highlighting your experience as a senior creative lead. We look forward to hearing from you!
score_reason: Senior remote role with strong brand creative fit, but agency delivery model and lack of integrated campaign/AI scope creates meaningful gap versus Travis's national CD background at major brands.

title: Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: Why We Exist More than 80% of Americans own a car and see it as a fundamental necessity, yet the experience of owning one is stuck in the 90s. Every part of the user journey (shopping for a car, insurance, repairs, etc) is complicated, fragmented, and time-consuming, and the costs are staggering. In 2025, the cost of owning a car was more than 20% of the average household income, after tax. At Jerry.ai, we are building the first app to manage it all and make car ownership accessible to all. Why We Need You Since we launched our app in 2019, we’ve reached 5M customers, raised over $240M in funding, scaled our annual revenue more than 70X, and became profitable in early 2024 – just 7 years into our operating history, in spite of challenging macro conditions and in a hyper-competitive $2T U.S. market. We did this with an exceptionally talented team of analytical thinkers who are brilliant at experimentation and growth optimization. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Car ownership is a stressful and emotionally charged topic for most people, and right now, our messaging is very transactional (e.g. click here, shop with us). We need you to change that. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Whether you’re crafting email sequences, push notifications, navigating a subreddit thread, or shaping how we talk about a new feature launch, you will be the voice of Jerry. And you will help us earn our brand and cultivate an enduring community. We spent the last 9 years building a product that people like, now we need to give them a reason to trust us and a reason to stay. Why You Will (or Won’t) Like Working Here Blank canvas: You won’t inherit a tone of voice or brand playbook. You will build it in partnership with our CEO, including defining our personality and voice across all organic surfaces. Hands-on: This is a senior level role in scope and impact, but you will be an IC with no direct reports on day one. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. Phase 1: Your sole focus will be overhauling our existing customer communications, including our CRM, email, SMS, and in-app copy. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. You can translate complex, dry, and even stressful topics (car insurance, loans, repairs) into copy that is engaging, human, and accessible. A T-Shaped marketer: Your deep expertise is in copywriting, audience development and product positioning. Horizontally, you have a great sense of the social media landscape and community dynamics (like Reddit). A first principles thinker: You avoid making assumptions. You know how to take qualitative data and user pain points and turn them into resonant, empathetic marketing copy. Comfortable with discomfort: You’ve weathered fast-growing startups before and can confidently push back on highly analytical, data-first teams to champion the unmeasurable va
score_reason: Senior creative scope on lifecycle/organic growth with strong emotional/messaging mandate, but DTC/growth-focused rather than integrated campaigns; no explicit remote confirmation or location details provided.

title: Senior Creative Strategist
description: If you have not directly led performance creative that scaled a brand from low–mid 7 figures to 8 or 9 figures in revenue, this role is not a fit. If you have, and want the next big growth + scale challenge, read on. About AutoReel AutoReel is the leading AI-powered platform that transforms real estate listing photos into cinematic property videos in minutes. We serve thousands of users — from independent agents to major brokerages. Our platform enables real estate professionals (realtors, brokers, real estate photographers and media companies) to create professional property videos without the high cost and time associated with traditional video production. We’re a small, fast-moving team that ships constantly, with the leadership team from world-class companies such as Facebook, Instagram, and Snapchat. We’re scaling rapidly and need a creative leader who can build the creative engine that fuels our paid growth across Meta Ads, YouTube, and beyond. About the Role We’re looking for a Head of Performance Creative to own the creative strategy and production behind AutoReel’s paid acquisition. This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll build the systems, build and manage a lean team (primarily offshore), and personally own the creative ads output that powers our growth. You’ll be responsible for producing 50-100+ video ads per week across channels, building scalable creative testing frameworks, and integrating AI tools into every stage of the workflow. This needs to be a thoughtful, scalable system that can support scaling up to 100-500 creatives weekly if needed. You understand how Meta and TikTok algorithms reward creative, and you know how to turn that knowledge into lower CPAs and higher ROAS. This isn’t a “big team, big budget” role. It’s a “scrappy, high-output, build-the-machine” role at a startup that’s growing fast and competing hard. What You’ll Do Own performance creative strategy: Set the creative direction and testing roadmap on Meta. You’ll decide what gets made, why, and how we measure success. Build and run the creative engine: Design the workflows, templates, feedback & learning systems that let us produce 50+ high-quality ad creatives per week without burning out – and support scaling up the number of creatives weekly as we grow. Lead with AI: Integrate AI tools into every stage of creative production — from ideation and scripting to editing and iteration. Maximize throughput while keeping quality high. Manage a small, high-performing team of creatives (as needed) Drive creative testing at scale: Build structured testing frameworks (hooks, formats, messaging angles, audiences) and turn data into actionable creative briefs. Partner with Growth: Work closely with our paid media buyer and creative production team to align creative strategy with campaign performance. Turn spend data into better ads. Know our customers deeply: Understand the real estate agent and photographer psyche — their fears, desires, and buying triggers — and translate that into ads that convert. Qualifications Direct owner or co‑owner of creative for at least one SaaS (or DTC) brand that grew from 7 figures to 8 or 9 figures in ARR/revenue while you were in the seat. 5+ years building and leading creative teams in performance marketing, growth, or digital advertising. Proven track record of producing high-volume performance creative on Meta Deep understanding of how platform algorithms evaluate and reward creative — you think in hooks, thumb-stop rates, and hold rates. Hands-on experience with AI tools and creative tools (video generation, script writing, image editing) and a strong opinion on how to integrate them into production workflows. Experience building creative testing systems and translating performance data into actionable creative strategy. Comfortable in a startup environment where you’ll build processes and systems from scratch, not inherit them. Strong editorial eye and ability to give fast, clear creative feedback. Bonus:
score_reason: Strong performance creative pedigree match and AI integration appeal, but DTC/SaaS performance focus and missing remote/salary details create uncertainty.

Our platform combines the best of AI and human intelligence to help contact centers discover customer insights and behavioral best practices, automate conversations and inefficient processes, and empower every team member to work smarter and faster. Born from the prestigious Stanford AI lab, Cresta's co-founder and chairman is Sebastian Thrun, the genius behind Google X, Waymo, Udacity, and more. Our leadership also includes CEO, Ping Wu, the co-founder of Google Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platform, and co-founder, Tim Shi, an early member of Open AI. Our valued customers include brands like Intuit, Cox Communications, Hilton, and Carmax and we’ve been recognized by Forbes and Bain Consulting as one of the top private AI companies in the world. Join us on this thrilling journey to revolutionize the workforce with AI. The future of work is here, and it's at Cresta. This job is perfect for a multi-talented creative thinker who is up for a challenge. title: Lead Strategist – UGC
description: About UGC Factory UGC Factory (www.ugcfactory.io) partners with fast-growing DTC brands to build high-converting, creator-led ads across paid social. We combine real customer journeys, rigorous performance analysis, and tight creative systems to ship UGC that actually moves MER, not just fills a content folder. Role UGC Factory is hiring a Lead Strategist to own the performance of all UGC and statics across our client roster. This person is the de-facto head of creative: the one who sits between data, strategy, scripts, creators, and editors, and is directly accountable for whether the ads work. This role replaces the founder on key client calls, leads ad review, and acts as the final backstop on creative quality and direction. Responsibilities Take end-to-end ownership for why ads work or don't work across multiple accounts. Review performance reports and audits, and distill them into clear creative direction per client. Attend and gradually lead client kickoffs and recurring syncs for key accounts. Translate messy client input into sharp creative strategy, including angles, frameworks, proof requirements, and UGC versus testimonial versus product-seeding plans. Call out and correct misaligned strategies when the creative approach does not fit the offer / SKU Take learnings from performance data and convert them into reusable patterns the team can apply across brands. Ensure those insights actually show up in briefs and not just in a single account silo. Refine AI-generated scripts and frameworks so every approved script reflects a high standard on hooks, angles, and structure. Requirements 3-5+ years driving paid social creative strategy for DTC brands, with significant Meta experience. Strong fluency in Ads Manager and in turning performance data into specific creative hypotheses. Proven ability to lead or heavily influence UGC and creator-led creative across multiple brands at once. Strong scripting instincts and the ability to quickly spot weak hooks, missing proof, and structural issues in UGC and testimonial content. Comfortable being client-facing, running calls, pushing back on misaligned strategies, and owning results. High attention to detail and a habit of catching misaligned frameworks, missing creator data, and bad ideas before they reach the client. Compensation & Commitment Base salary: $60,000–$80,000 Performance incentive: Up to additional $40,000/yr tied to client retention & portfolio winner rate Remote-friendly; available during US business hours
